jokes.text: Ah, another beautiful day in the womb.
jokes.setup: Homer contentedly lying in bed
jokes.punchline: Comparing his warm bed to being in the womb
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's childlike regression and sets up his resistance to adult responsibilities. Original metaphor with great economy.

jokes.text: I'm all naked and wet!
jokes.setup: Homer being pulled from his warm bed
jokes.punchline: Continuing the womb metaphor with birth imagery
jokes.explanation: Extends the womb joke effectively with uncomfortable birth imagery that makes the audience cringe-laugh.

jokes.text: Oh, stupid, itchy church pants. One size fits all, my butt!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to get dressed for church
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'one size fits all' applied to his butt
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific complaint with mild wordplay on sizing labels.

jokes.text: 'Resting' hungover, 'resting' got fired? Help me out here.
jokes.setup: Marge euphemistically saying Homer is 'resting'
jokes.punchline: Bart cutting through the euphemism with specific possibilities
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bart's cynical awareness of his father's patterns. Sharp structure and delivery.

jokes.text: Ahhh, I'm just a big, toasty cinnamon bun. I never wanna leave this bed.
jokes.setup: Homer luxuriating in his warm bed
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a cinnamon bun
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer-esque food metaphor that captures his comfort and resistance to responsibility.

jokes.text: I'm whizzin' with the door open, and I love it.
jokes.setup: Homer alone in the house needing to use bathroom
jokes.punchline: Celebrating minor domestic freedom
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer joy in small rebellions against social norms. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: You bet your sweet... ass!
jokes.setup: Radio DJ saying 'You bet your sweet...' then cutting to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer completing the censored phrase
jokes.explanation: Clean edit joke playing with broadcast standards. Simple but effective timing.

jokes.text: The perfect chance to make my patented, space-age... out-of-this-world moon waffles.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to cook breakfast
jokes.punchline: Overly grandiose description of simple waffles
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to make mundane things sound epic. Slightly verbose but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oooh, waffle runoff.
jokes.setup: Homer cooking waffles
jokes.punchline: Treating spilled batter like a bonus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment finding joy in cooking mishaps. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Mmm, fattening.
jokes.setup: Homer eating his elaborate breakfast
jokes.punchline: Appreciating food specifically because it's unhealthy
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer food appreciation. Perfect character integration with maximum economy.

jokes.text: Ahhh, I'm there.
jokes.setup: Reverend describing the fiery cauldron of hell while Homer is warm and comfortable
jokes.punchline: Homer relating to the description positively
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic juxtaposition between hell's description and Homer's comfort. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: The door's frozen shut, and it's the only way out!
jokes.setup: The church service has ended
jokes.punchline: Everyone is trapped because the door is frozen
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup that creates ironic contrast with Homer's comfortable situation

jokes.text: Homer gasps and immediately knows the radio trivia answer
jokes.setup: Radio contest about obscure political album
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow knows this random piece of trivia
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's random knowledge base - sets up his lucky day theme

jokes.text: This Things I Believe
jokes.setup: Radio contest asking for album title
jokes.punchline: Homer's grammatically incorrect answer
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's poor education but somehow getting the answer right. Mild character comedy.

jokes.text: Miracles are your department, Reverend.
jokes.setup: Reverend asking Willie about the frozen door
jokes.punchline: Willie deflecting responsibility with dry humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie deadpan response highlighting church/practical reality divide. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Card table for sale. Top badly damaged. Leg missing. Otherwise fine. One dollar or best offer.
jokes.setup: Reverend reading bulletin to kill time
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pathetic description of worthless table
jokes.explanation: Good escalating description of worthless item. Shows church community's modest circumstances.

jokes.text: Moe is their leader.
jokes.setup: Watching Three Stooges with Moe giving orders
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious observation delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Homer stating the obvious about Three Stooges hierarchy. Simple character moment showing his literal thinking.

jokes.text: Come on, TV. Give me some of that sweet, sweet pap.
jokes.setup: Homer channel surfing for entertainment
jokes.punchline: Directly acknowledging and requesting mindless content
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-aware request for mindless entertainment. Great phrase 'sweet, sweet pap' with good character integration.

jokes.text: Yes!
jokes.setup: Public affairs program is interrupted
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates getting football instead of politics
jokes.explanation: Economical reaction that perfectly captures Homer's preferences and attitude toward intellectual content

jokes.text: Give it a little more gas. [Pedal Banging] No, no, that's too much. You know what I think would help? What? What would help? Nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer offering car troubleshooting advice
jokes.punchline: Admitting he has no helpful suggestions
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Homer building up to admitting uselessness. Good structure and honest character moment.

jokes.text: I found... a penny! Could this be the best day of my life?
jokes.setup: Homer having a great day skipping church
jokes.punchline: Finding a penny as potential life pinnacle
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple pleasures and low standards perfectly captured. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Like fun I don't. Marge, I'm never going to church again!
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer doesn't mean his anti-church sentiment
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicting her and escalating
jokes.explanation: Homer's stubborn escalation undercutting Marge's damage control. Good marital dynamic.

jokes.text: No. No, no, no, no, no, no. Well, yes.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer is giving up his faith
jokes.punchline: Homer's multiple denials followed by admission
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer indecision pattern with good timing. Shows his internal conflict while being funny.

jokes.text: I mean, isn't God everywhere? Amen, brother.
jokes.setup: Homer making theological argument against church attendance
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately supporting dad's self-serving logic
jokes.explanation: Bart immediately supporting any excuse to skip church. Good family dynamic and character consistency.

jokes.text: And what if we pick the wrong religion? Every week we're just making God madder and madder. Testify!
jokes.setup: Homer's theological reasoning about church attendance
jokes.punchline: Worrying about making God incrementally angrier, Bart's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist logic about religious risk-taking. Bart's 'Testify!' adds perfect punctuation.

jokes.text: Thou has forsaken my church! Well, kind of, but-- But what?
jokes.setup: God appearing to confront Homer about skipping church
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting to it
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual admission to God while being confronted. Perfect character moment showing his honest directness even with the Almighty.

jokes.text: You know, sometimes... even I'd rather be watching football. Does St. Louis still have a team? No, they moved to Phoenix.
jokes.setup: God admitting to preferring football to church
jokes.punchline: Casual sports conversation between God and Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant humanization of God through sports small talk. Subverts expectations while maintaining Homer's everyman relatability.

jokes.text: Hmm, you've got a point there.
jokes.setup: Homer explains why he shouldn't have to go to church
jokes.punchline: God immediately agrees with Homer's logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - God being reasonable and agreeing with Homer's complaints about organized religion. Very original concept

jokes.text: You know what I really hate about church? Those boring sermons. Oh, I couldn't agree more. That Reverend Lovejoy really displeases me. I think I'll give him a canker sore.
jokes.setup: Homer and God bonding over complaints about church
jokes.punchline: God's petty divine punishment for bad sermons
jokes.explanation: God as petty as any person, using divine power for minor revenge. Perfect blend of absurd and relatable.

jokes.text: Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to appear on a tortilla in Mexico.
jokes.setup: God ending his conversation with Homer
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to appearing on food items as divine manifestation
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of religious phenomena with God treating miracle appearances as routine appointments. Excellent economy and originality.

jokes.text: Dad, can I ask you a question? / Sure, honey. / Why are you dedicating your life to blasphemy?
jokes.setup: Lisa's innocent setup for a question
jokes.punchline: Her sophisticated theological accusation delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - precocious vocabulary in innocent delivery, great misdirection

jokes.text: Don't worry, sweetheart. If I'm wrong, I'll recant on my deathbed.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about Homer's blasphemy
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical approach to hedging religious bets
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about deathbed conversions as insurance policy. Shows Homer's practical cynicism beneath his spiritual rebellion.

jokes.text: He appeared before me in a dream... and I knew that was special because I usually dream about naked... Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his divine vision to Reverend Lovejoy
jokes.punchline: TMI about his usual dream content
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overshare creating awkwardness. Good character consistency with his lack of social filters.

jokes.text: Perfect teeth, nice smell, a class act all the way.
jokes.setup: Asked what God looked like in his dream
jokes.punchline: Describing God like a used car salesman or date
jokes.explanation: Homer's shallow criteria for judging even the divine. Perfectly captures his materialistic worldview.

jokes.text: 'And he left them and went out of the city into Bethany and he lodged there'? Yeah. Think about it.
jokes.setup: Reverend citing specific Bible verse as counterargument
jokes.punchline: Homer pretending random verse supports his position
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident misuse of scripture he clearly doesn't understand. Great character moment showing his fake-it-til-you-make-it approach.

jokes.text: The, uh, Feast of Maximum Occupancy.
jokes.setup: Homer calling in sick for religious holiday
jokes.punchline: Making up holiday name from fire code sign
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvised fake holiday name. Perfect blend of Homer's laziness and accidental creativity.

jokes.text: Sorry, Homer. I was born a snake handler, and I'll die a snake handler.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to recruit Moe to his religion
jokes.punchline: Moe's commitment to dangerous religious practice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line showing his stubborn loyalty to dangerous tradition. Great character voice and unexpected detail.

jokes.text: Kids, let me tell you about another so-called 'wicked' guy. He had long hair and some wild ideas... and he didn't always do what other people thought was right. And that man's name was-- I forget. But the point is-- I forget that too.
jokes.setup: Homer building up to inspirational story about unconventional figure
jokes.punchline: Forgetting both the name and the point mid-speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment of confident ignorance. Great escalating failure structure showing his incompetent parenting attempts.

jokes.text: He used to drive that blue car.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember the inspirational figure
jokes.punchline: Remembering irrelevant mundane detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer brain prioritizing trivial details over important ones. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: There you go again. Always taking someone else's side-- Flanders, the water department, God.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about Marge's loyalties
jokes.punchline: Lumping God in with Flanders and utilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation putting God on same level as mundane annoyances. Perfect Homer self-centeredness and jealousy.

jokes.text: Our unabashed dictionary defines I.U.D. as... 'love springs internal.' I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Homer reading adult magazine euphemisms
jokes.punchline: Not understanding obvious sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's sexual naivety despite reading adult material. Consistent with his childlike understanding.

jokes.text: Last year, tornadoes claimed the lives of 75 Jewish clowns. The worst incident was during our convention in Lubbock, Texas. There were floppy shoes and rainbow wigs everywhere. It was terrible.
jokes.setup: Doorbell charity solicitation
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic natural disaster affecting very specific group
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdist humor about hyper-specific tragedy. The visual detail makes it memorably grotesque and funny.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Is this a religious thing? / A religious clown thing, yes. / Sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer's suspicion about the charity's religious nature
jokes.punchline: His immediate rejection once religion is confirmed
jokes.explanation: Homer's consistent rejection of anything religious, even absurd charities

jokes.text: No offense, Apu, but when they were handing out religions... you must've been out taking a whiz.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining his Hindu shrine
jokes.punchline: Homer's ignorant dismissal of Hinduism
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual religious bigotry played for cringe humor. Shows his ignorant worldview while making audience uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Boy, everyone is stupid except me.
jokes.setup: Homer having dismissed everyone's religious beliefs
jokes.punchline: His oblivious self-satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer arrogance and lack of self-awareness. Maximum economy capturing his entire worldview in six words.

jokes.text: D'oh! The song, the song. [Singing] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember fire safety song during actual fire
jokes.punchline: Failing at remembering and giving up with signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's incompetence in crisis combined with signature catchphrase. Good character consistency in emergency situation.

jokes.text: Little Jamshed, the store is in your hands. How I have waited for this day. [Shotgun cocking]
jokes.setup: Apu leaving child in charge of store during emergency
jokes.punchline: Child's sinister excitement and weapon sound
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child's eagerness for power and violence. Great escalation from innocent moment to threatening.

jokes.text: You ducks are really trying my patience! But you're so cute.
jokes.setup: Fire chief frustrated with ducks blocking fire truck
jokes.punchline: Immediately softening due to their cuteness
jokes.explanation: Nice emotional whiplash from authority figure. Shows human weakness overriding professional duty.

jokes.text: Help! Help! [Laughing] That's right, old friend.
jokes.setup: Flanders calling for help while saving Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous amusement at Flanders' danger
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfishness even when being rescued. Dark character moment showing his lack of empathy.

jokes.text: Hey, what are these axes for? I don't know. Chopping stuff. Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about fire department equipment
jokes.punchline: Firefighter's unhelpfully obvious answer, Homer's satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Simple exchange showing Homer's literal thinking and firefighter's blunt honesty. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Well, the Picasso, my collection of classic cars-- Sorry. This policy only covers actual losses, not made-up stuff.
jokes.setup: Homer listing expensive possessions lost in fire
jokes.punchline: Insurance agent calling out his obvious lies
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's insurance fraud attempt. Shows his opportunistic nature and the agent's world-weary experience.

jokes.text: You know, I have a feeling there's a lesson here. Yes, the lesson is-- No, don't tell me. I'll get it.
jokes.setup: Homer recognizes there should be a lesson from his experience
jokes.punchline: He cuts off the explanation to figure it out himself
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's desire to learn while maintaining his stubbornness about accepting help

jokes.text: O Spiteful One, show me who to smite, and they shall be 'smoten.'
jokes.setup: Homer learning lesson about God's vengeance
jokes.punchline: Wrong conjugation of 'smite'
jokes.explanation: Homer's poor grammar while trying to sound biblical. Good character consistency with his education level.

jokes.text: Hindu. There are 700 million of us. Aw, that's super.
jokes.setup: Reverend referring to Apu as 'miscellaneous' religion
jokes.punchline: Apu's correction and Reverend's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Great cultural insensitivity joke with Reverend's patronizing dismissal of major world religion. Good character voice for both.

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, Homer. Nine out of ten religions fail in their first year.
jokes.setup: Homer's failed attempt at starting his own religion
jokes.punchline: God treating religions like business startups
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke treating religion formation like business entrepreneurship. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: You can't wait six months?
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to know meaning of life immediately
jokes.punchline: God implying Homer only has six months to live
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's mortality delivered casually. Perfect economy and great misdirection setup.

jokes.text: Well, okay. The meaning of life is--
jokes.setup: God agrees to reveal the meaning of life
jokes.punchline: Credits roll before he can finish
jokes.explanation: Classic cutoff ending that teases the ultimate answer but never delivers it, perfectly structured frustration

jokes.text: The meaning of life is-- [Cut to credits]
jokes.setup: God about to reveal ultimate meaning of existence
jokes.punchline: Episode ending right before the revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending gag using episode structure as punchline. Great economy and audience frustration as comedy.
